instruction: Implement a C++ database access layer using RAII principles for connection and transaction management. Show how to safely execute prepared statements against PostgreSQL using libpq, handle connection pooling with a thread-safe pool, and ensure resources are properly released even during exceptions.
output:
<think>
Database access in C++ demands careful resource management. Key design decisions:

1. **RAII for connections**: Wrap `PGconn*` in a class whose destructor calls `PQfinish()`. This guarantees cleanup even if exceptions propagate through the call stack. Move semantics allow transfer without copying.

2. **RAII for transactions**: A `Transaction` class calls `BEGIN` on construction and `ROLLBACK` on destruction. Explicit `commit()` sets a flag preventing the rollback. This ensures no dangling transactions.

3. **Connection pool**: Thread-safe pool using `std::mutex` and `std::condition_variable`. Connections are checked out/in. Pool tracks idle and active connections. Max connections limit prevents overwhelming the database.

4. **Prepared statements**: `PQprepare()` pre-parses SQL on the server. Reuse across queries on the same connection saves parsing time. The statement cache is per-connection (PostgreSQL requirement).

5. **Error handling**: Check `PQresultStatus()` after every query. Map PostgreSQL error codes (SQLSTATE) to C++ exceptions. The result wrapper (`PGresult*`) also uses RAII.

6. **Thread safety**: The connection pool is thread-safe. Individual connections are NOT thread-safe (PostgreSQL limitation). Each thread checks out its own connection.
</think>

```cpp
#include <libpq-fe.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>
#include <mutex>
#include <condition_variable>
#include <queue>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <functional>
#include <optional>
#include <sstream>

// --- RAII Result Wrapper ---
class PgResult {
    PGresult* result_ = nullptr;
public:
    explicit PgResult(PGresult* r) : result_(r) {}
    ~PgResult() { if (result_) PQclear(result_); }
    PgResult(PgResult&& o) noexcept : result_(o.result_) { o.result_ = nullptr; }
    PgResult& operator=(PgResult&&) = delete;
    PgResult(const PgResult&) = delete;

    PGresult* get() const { return result_; }
    ExecStatusType status() const { return PQresultStatus(result_); }
    int rows() const { return PQntuples(result_); }
    int cols() const { return PQnfields(result_); }
    const char* value(int row, int col) const { return PQgetvalue(result_, row, col); }
    bool isNull(int row, int col) const { return PQgetisnull(result_, row, col); }
    std::string error() const { return PQresultErrorMessage(result_); }

    void ensureOk() const {
        auto s = status();
        if (s != PGRES_COMMAND_OK && s != PGRES_TUPLES_OK) {
            throw std::runtime_error("Query failed: " + error());
        }
    }
};

// --- RAII Connection ---
class PgConnection {
    PGconn* conn_ = nullptr;
    std::vector<std::string> prepared_;

public:
    explicit PgConnection(const std::string& conninfo) {
        conn_ = PQconnectdb(conninfo.c_str());
        if (PQstatus(conn_) != CONNECTION_OK) {
            std::string err = PQerrorMessage(conn_);
            PQfinish(conn_);
            conn_ = nullptr;
            throw std::runtime_error("Connection failed: " + err);
        }
    }

    ~PgConnection() { if (conn_) PQfinish(conn_); }

    PgConnection(PgConnection&& o) noexcept
        : conn_(o.conn_), prepared_(std::move(o.prepared_)) {
        o.conn_ = nullptr;
    }

    PgConnection(const PgConnection&) = delete;
    PgConnection& operator=(const PgConnection&) = delete;

    bool isAlive() const {
        return conn_ && PQstatus(conn_) == CONNECTION_OK;
    }

    void reset() {
        PQreset(conn_);
        prepared_.clear();
    }

    PgResult exec(const std::string& sql) {
        PgResult r(PQexec(conn_, sql.c_str()));
        r.ensureOk();
        return r;
    }

    PgResult execParams(const std::string& sql, const std::vector<std::string>& params) {
        std::vector<const char*> values;
        for (const auto& p : params) values.push_back(p.c_str());

        PgResult r(PQexecParams(conn_, sql.c_str(),
            static_cast<int>(params.size()), nullptr,
            values.data(), nullptr, nullptr, 0));
        r.ensureOk();
        return r;
    }

    void prepare(const std::string& name, const std::string& sql, int nparams) {
        PgResult r(PQprepare(conn_, name.c_str(), sql.c_str(), nparams, nullptr));
        r.ensureOk();
        prepared_.push_back(name);
    }

    PgResult execPrepared(const std::string& name, const std::vector<std::string>& params) {
        std::vector<const char*> values;
        for (const auto& p : params) values.push_back(p.c_str());

        PgResult r(PQexecPrepared(conn_, name.c_str(),
            static_cast<int>(params.size()),
            values.data(), nullptr, nullptr, 0));
        r.ensureOk();
        return r;
    }

    PGconn* raw() { return conn_; }
};

// --- RAII Transaction ---
class Transaction {
    PgConnection& conn_;
    bool committed_ = false;

public:
    explicit Transaction(PgConnection& conn) : conn_(conn) {
        conn_.exec("BEGIN");
    }

    ~Transaction() {
        if (!committed_) {
            try { conn_.exec("ROLLBACK"); } catch (...) {}
        }
    }

    void commit() {
        conn_.exec("COMMIT");
        committed_ = true;
    }

    PgConnection& connection() { return conn_; }

    Transaction(const Transaction&) = delete;
    Transaction& operator=(const Transaction&) = delete;
};

// --- Thread-Safe Connection Pool ---
class ConnectionPool {
    std::string conninfo_;
    size_t max_size_;
    std::queue<std::unique_ptr<PgConnection>> idle_;
    size_t active_count_ = 0;
    std::mutex mutex_;
    std::condition_variable cv_;
    bool closed_ = false;

public:
    ConnectionPool(const std::string& conninfo, size_t max_size)
        : conninfo_(conninfo), max_size_(max_size) {}

    ~ConnectionPool() { close(); }

    class Lease {
        ConnectionPool& pool_;
        std::unique_ptr<PgConnection> conn_;
    public:
        Lease(ConnectionPool& pool, std::unique_ptr<PgConnection> conn)
            : pool_(pool), conn_(std::move(conn)) {}
        ~Lease() { if (conn_) pool_.release(std::move(conn_)); }
        Lease(Lease&& o) noexcept : pool_(o.pool_), conn_(std::move(o.conn_)) {}
        PgConnection& operator*() { return *conn_; }
        PgConnection* operator->() { return conn_.get(); }
    };

    Lease acquire() {
        std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);

        while (idle_.empty() && active_count_ >= max_size_ && !closed_) {
            cv_.wait(lock);
        }

        if (closed_) throw std::runtime_error("Pool is closed");

        std::unique_ptr<PgConnection> conn;
        if (!idle_.empty()) {
            conn = std::move(idle_.front());
            idle_.pop();
            if (!conn->isAlive()) {
                conn->reset();
            }
        } else {
            conn = std::make_unique<PgConnection>(conninfo_);
        }

        active_count_++;
        return Lease(*this, std::move(conn));
    }

    void release(std::unique_ptr<PgConnection> conn) {
        std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
        active_count_--;
        if (!closed_ && conn->isAlive()) {
            idle_.push(std::move(conn));
        }
        cv_.notify_one();
    }

    void close() {
        std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
        closed_ = true;
        while (!idle_.empty()) idle_.pop();
        cv_.notify_all();
    }

    size_t activeCount() const { return active_count_; }
    size_t idleCount() { std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); return idle_.size(); }
};

// --- User Repository using Pool ---
struct User {
    std::string id;
    std::string email;
    std::string name;
    bool is_active;
};

class UserRepository {
    ConnectionPool& pool_;
public:
    explicit UserRepository(ConnectionPool& pool) : pool_(pool) {}

    std::optional<User> findById(const std::string& id) {
        auto conn = pool_.acquire();
        auto result = conn->execParams(
            "SELECT id, email, name, is_active FROM users WHERE id = $1",
            {id});

        if (result.rows() == 0) return std::nullopt;

        return User{
            result.value(0, 0),
            result.value(0, 1),
            result.value(0, 2),
            std::string(result.value(0, 3)) == "t",
        };
    }

    User create(const std::string& email, const std::string& name) {
        auto conn = pool_.acquire();
        Transaction tx(*conn);

        auto result = conn->execParams(
            "INSERT INTO users (id, email, name, is_active, created_at) "
            "VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), $1, $2, true, NOW()) "
            "RETURNING id, email, name, is_active",
            {email, name});

        tx.commit();

        return User{
            result.value(0, 0),
            result.value(0, 1),
            result.value(0, 2),
            true,
        };
    }

    std::vector<User> list(int limit, int offset) {
        auto conn = pool_.acquire();
        auto result = conn->execParams(
            "SELECT id, email, name, is_active FROM users "
            "ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2",
            {std::to_string(limit), std::to_string(offset)});

        std::vector<User> users;
        for (int i = 0; i < result.rows(); i++) {
            users.push_back({
                result.value(i, 0),
                result.value(i, 1),
                result.value(i, 2),
                std::string(result.value(i, 3)) == "t",
            });
        }
        return users;
    }
};

// --- Usage ---
int main() {
    ConnectionPool pool("host=localhost dbname=myapp", 10);
    UserRepository users(pool);

    auto user = users.create("test@example.com", "Test User");
    auto found = users.findById(user.id);
    auto list = users.list(20, 0);

    return 0;
}
```

instruction: Design a C++ database abstraction that supports multiple backends (SQLite and PostgreSQL) using compile-time polymorphism with templates and CRTP. Show how to write backend-agnostic query code, handle prepared statements across backends, and manage transactions with RAII guarantees.
output:
<think>
A multi-backend database abstraction in C++ needs to balance performance (no virtual dispatch overhead) with flexibility:

1. **CRTP for static polymorphism**: The base `Database<Derived>` class defines the interface. Concrete backends (`SqliteBackend`, `PgBackend`) inherit from it. Method calls are resolved at compile time through `static_cast<Derived*>(this)->method()`, zero overhead.

2. **Backend-agnostic queries**: A `Query` class that builds SQL strings. Different backends may have different parameter placeholder syntax (`?` for SQLite, `$1` for PostgreSQL). The query builder adapts based on the backend type.

3. **Prepared statements**: Each backend has different prepared statement APIs. The CRTP interface defines `prepare()`, `bind()`, `execute()`. SQLite uses `sqlite3_stmt*`, PostgreSQL uses `PQprepare/PQexecPrepared`. Template specialization handles the differences.

4. **RAII transactions**: Same pattern as before — begin in constructor, rollback in destructor unless committed. The transaction class is templated on the backend type.

5. **Type-safe parameter binding**: Use variadic templates to bind parameters with proper type checking. Each backend converts C++ types to their native format.

6. **Result iteration**: A `ResultSet` class with `next()` and column accessors. Template specialization per backend wraps the native result object.
</think>

```cpp
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <sstream>
#include <functional>

// --- Backend-Agnostic Query Builder ---

enum class ParamStyle { QuestionMark, Dollar };

class QueryBuilder {
    std::string sql_;
    int param_count_ = 0;
    ParamStyle style_;

public:
    explicit QueryBuilder(ParamStyle style = ParamStyle::Dollar) : style_(style) {}

    QueryBuilder& raw(const std::string& sql) { sql_ += sql; return *this; }

    QueryBuilder& param() {
        param_count_++;
        if (style_ == ParamStyle::Dollar) {
            sql_ += "$" + std::to_string(param_count_);
        } else {
            sql_ += "?";
        }
        return *this;
    }

    QueryBuilder& select(const std::string& cols) {
        sql_ = "SELECT " + cols;
        return *this;
    }

    QueryBuilder& from(const std::string& table) {
        sql_ += " FROM " + table;
        return *this;
    }

    QueryBuilder& where(const std::string& condition) {
        sql_ += " WHERE " + condition;
        return *this;
    }

    const std::string& build() const { return sql_; }
    int paramCount() const { return param_count_; }
};

// --- CRTP Base ---

template<typename Derived>
class Database {
public:
    void connect(const std::string& conninfo) {
        static_cast<Derived*>(this)->connectImpl(conninfo);
    }

    void disconnect() {
        static_cast<Derived*>(this)->disconnectImpl();
    }

    bool isConnected() const {
        return static_cast<const Derived*>(this)->isConnectedImpl();
    }

    auto execute(const std::string& sql) {
        return static_cast<Derived*>(this)->executeImpl(sql);
    }

    auto executeParams(const std::string& sql,
                       const std::vector<std::string>& params) {
        return static_cast<Derived*>(this)->executeParamsImpl(sql, params);
    }

    void beginTransaction() {
        static_cast<Derived*>(this)->executeImpl("BEGIN");
    }

    void commit() {
        static_cast<Derived*>(this)->executeImpl("COMMIT");
    }

    void rollback() {
        static_cast<Derived*>(this)->executeImpl("ROLLBACK");
    }
};

// --- Generic Result Set ---

template<typename BackendResult>
class ResultSet {
    BackendResult result_;
    int current_row_ = -1;

public:
    explicit ResultSet(BackendResult result) : result_(std::move(result)) {}

    bool next() {
        current_row_++;
        return current_row_ < result_.rowCount();
    }

    std::string getString(int col) const {
        return result_.getString(current_row_, col);
    }

    int getInt(int col) const {
        return std::stoi(result_.getString(current_row_, col));
    }

    double getDouble(int col) const {
        return std::stod(result_.getString(current_row_, col));
    }

    bool isNull(int col) const {
        return result_.isNull(current_row_, col);
    }

    int rowCount() const { return result_.rowCount(); }
    int columnCount() const { return result_.columnCount(); }
};

// --- RAII Transaction (templated on backend) ---

template<typename DB>
class Transaction {
    DB& db_;
    bool committed_ = false;

public:
    explicit Transaction(DB& db) : db_(db) {
        db_.beginTransaction();
    }

    ~Transaction() {
        if (!committed_) {
            try { db_.rollback(); } catch (...) {}
        }
    }

    void commit() {
        db_.commit();
        committed_ = true;
    }

    DB& database() { return db_; }

    Transaction(const Transaction&) = delete;
    Transaction& operator=(const Transaction&) = delete;
};

// --- Simulated SQLite Backend ---

struct SqliteResult {
    std::vector<std::vector<std::string>> data;
    int cols = 0;

    int rowCount() const { return static_cast<int>(data.size()); }
    int columnCount() const { return cols; }
    std::string getString(int row, int col) const { return data[row][col]; }
    bool isNull(int row, int col) const { return data[row][col].empty(); }
};

class SqliteBackend : public Database<SqliteBackend> {
    bool connected_ = false;
    std::string path_;

public:
    static constexpr ParamStyle paramStyle = ParamStyle::QuestionMark;

    void connectImpl(const std::string& path) {
        path_ = path;
        connected_ = true;
        // sqlite3_open(path.c_str(), &db_);
    }

    void disconnectImpl() { connected_ = false; }
    bool isConnectedImpl() const { return connected_; }

    ResultSet<SqliteResult> executeImpl(const std::string& sql) {
        // sqlite3_exec simulation
        return ResultSet<SqliteResult>(SqliteResult{});
    }

    ResultSet<SqliteResult> executeParamsImpl(
        const std::string& sql,
        const std::vector<std::string>& params
    ) {
        // sqlite3_prepare_v2 + bind + step simulation
        return ResultSet<SqliteResult>(SqliteResult{});
    }
};

// --- Simulated PostgreSQL Backend ---

struct PgResult {
    std::vector<std::vector<std::string>> data;
    int cols = 0;

    int rowCount() const { return static_cast<int>(data.size()); }
    int columnCount() const { return cols; }
    std::string getString(int row, int col) const { return data[row][col]; }
    bool isNull(int row, int col) const { return data[row][col].empty(); }
};

class PgBackend : public Database<PgBackend> {
    bool connected_ = false;

public:
    static constexpr ParamStyle paramStyle = ParamStyle::Dollar;

    void connectImpl(const std::string& conninfo) {
        connected_ = true;
        // PQconnectdb simulation
    }

    void disconnectImpl() { connected_ = false; }
    bool isConnectedImpl() const { return connected_; }

    ResultSet<PgResult> executeImpl(const std::string& sql) {
        return ResultSet<PgResult>(PgResult{});
    }

    ResultSet<PgResult> executeParamsImpl(
        const std::string& sql,
        const std::vector<std::string>& params
    ) {
        return ResultSet<PgResult>(PgResult{});
    }
};

// --- Backend-Agnostic Service ---

template<typename DB>
class UserService {
    DB& db_;

public:
    explicit UserService(DB& db) : db_(db) {}

    void createUser(const std::string& email, const std::string& name) {
        Transaction<DB> tx(db_);

        QueryBuilder qb(DB::paramStyle);
        qb.raw("INSERT INTO users (email, name) VALUES (");
        qb.param();
        qb.raw(", ");
        qb.param();
        qb.raw(")");

        db_.executeParams(qb.build(), {email, name});
        tx.commit();
    }

    auto findByEmail(const std::string& email) {
        QueryBuilder qb(DB::paramStyle);
        qb.select("id, email, name")
          .from("users")
          .where("email = ");
        qb.param();

        return db_.executeParams(qb.build(), {email});
    }
};

// --- Usage: same code, different backends ---
int main() {
    // SQLite version
    SqliteBackend sqlite;
    sqlite.connect("app.db");
    UserService<SqliteBackend> sqliteUsers(sqlite);
    sqliteUsers.createUser("test@test.com", "Test");

    // PostgreSQL version
    PgBackend pg;
    pg.connect("host=localhost dbname=app");
    UserService<PgBackend> pgUsers(pg);
    pgUsers.createUser("test@test.com", "Test");

    return 0;
}
```
